pi_items.metadata_value: Doll R, Hill AB. Br Med J 1956 (Reconstructed)
pi_items.verified_value: Doll, R., & Hill, A. B. (1956). Lung cancer and other causes of death in relation to smoking; a second report on the mortality of British doctors. British Medical Journal, 2(5001), 1071-1081.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses a reconstructed dataset based on the authentic Doll and Hill 1956 study published in the British Medical Journal. The study is a real, landmark epidemiological investigation of British doctors' smoking habits and mortality. However, the expected SMR range in the metadata (14.5-14.9) contradicts the actual SMR value found in published sources for current cigarette smokers, which is 15.9 (based on 218 observed deaths vs. 13.7 expected deaths). The expected total expected deaths range (10.2-10.5) could not be verified independently since the reconstructed CSV data files are not accessible in the Linux environment. The dataset is synthetic/reconstructed, not the original raw data from the 1956 study.
